meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: SDA Awards Contracts for 36 Golden Dome Missile Tracking Satellites
immediate.summary: The Space Development Agency announced contracts worth $1.75 billion to build 36 satellites in support of the Pentagon's Golden Dome missile defense shield. The post SDA Awards Contracts for 36 Golden Dome Missile Tracking Satellites appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Reditus readies first launch of its re-entry vehicle/hypersonic target
immediate.summary: The Missile Defense Agency is evaluating the companys ENOS spacecraft as a hypersonic target/testbed under the SHIELD contract, said Reditus CEO Stef Crum.

immediate.title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
immediate.summary: L3Harris Technologies is receiving $955 million and Sierra Space $798 million under fixed price Other Transaction Authority (OTA) rapid prototyping contracts for an expedited launch of Tranche 3, Tracking Layer satellites [] The post L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.summary: The Space Forces TacRS program has at least three more Victus demonstration projects in the works, with a total of $300 million requested from fiscal 2027 to 2031.

immediate.title: Canada Inks $564 Million Joint Strike Missile Deal with Kongsberg
immediate.summary: The Joint Strike Missile will equip Canada’s F-35 fleet, giving it the ability to carry internally stand-off weapons. Canada has chosen Kongsberg’s Joint Strike Missile (JSM) as its new air-to-surface standoff weapon. The $564 million award, according to a Jul. 8, 2026 press release from the Norwegian company, makes Canada the sixth operator of the []

immediate.title: Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic
immediate.summary: Voyager Technologies has completed its acquisition of lunar infrastructure company Astrobotic Technology, weeks after Astrobotic won two NASA lunar lander missions. The post Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures mixed as traders lift Fed rate hike bets ahead of key inflation data
Wilsbach: Active Airmen Must Have Body Composition Scores by July 31
immediate.summary: Active-Duty Airmen must ensure they have a waist-to-height ratio assessment in their official record within the next two weeks, Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Kenneth S. Wilsbach said in a July 13 letter to the force focused on physical fitness and appearance standards. The post Wilsbach: Active Airmen Must Have Body Composition Scores by July 31 appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Russia's launching a NASA astronaut and 2 cosmonauts to the International Space Station on July 14: Watch it live
immediate.summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon and two cosmonauts will launch toward the International Space Station atop a Russian rocket on Tuesday (July 14), and you can watch it live.

immediate.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham, 33-Year USAF Vet, Dies
immediate.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), one of the most influential Airmen to ever serve in Congress, died July 12 after a sudden cardiac event. He was 71. The post Sen. Lindsey Graham, 33-Year USAF Vet, Dies appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA’s Artemis III Flight Hardware Stacks Up at Kennedy
immediate.summary: It is full steam ahead at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida as teams process and assemble flight hardware in preparation for the upcoming crewed Artemis III mission. Less than three months after the safe return of the Artemis II crew, teams began stacking the twin SLS (Space Launch System) solid rocket booster segments inside []

immediate.title: Software Sold Separately: What the Air Force’s New Approach for CCAs Means
immediate.summary: The Air Force’s “software sold separately” plan to buy critical autonomy software decoupled from the physical air vehicle for its nascent Collaborative Combat Aircraft fleet could help the drones evolve quickly, foster competition, and avoid vendor lock-in, experts and service officials told Air Space Forces Magazine. The post Software Sold Separately: What the Air Force’s New Approach for CCAs Means appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
immediate.summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon poses in a spacesuit for a portrait at NASAs Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas on Jan. 8, 2026. Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition []

immediate.title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
immediate.summary: The FCC said concerns about how the spacecraft will impact the environment and astronomy are outside of the Commission’s scope. The post Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: The FCC just gave Reflect Orbital permission to launch its 1st space mirror to orbit. Tens of thousands more could follow
immediate.summary: Reflect Orbital just got approval to launch its first light-directing space mirror, which it plans to do later this year. Tens of thousands more such craft could follow over the next 10 years.

same_day.title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
same_day.summary: European government space spending jumped 12% to about $15.4 billion in 2025, according to a July 13 European Space Agency report, bucking a 3% global decline on the back of rising national defense budgets. The post Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets
same_day.summary: For many years Europe organized space around clear distinctions. Civil programs were separated from defense. Commercial companies served institutional customers. Governments developed sovereign capabilities while industry provided technology and services. [] The post The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets appeared first on Via Satellite .

same_day.summary: The Defense Department will keep cybersecurity self-assessments, but will not require third-party certifications as planned.

same_day.title: Lawmakers demand Pentagon release findings from probe of Iran school strike
same_day.summary: Lawmakers demanded Monday that the Pentagon release its findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike at a girls’ school in Iran.

same_day.title: Pentagon announces ‘immediate suspension’ of CMMC Phase II mandates
same_day.summary: Top Pentagon officials said as currently executed, CMMC is too prohibitively burdensome on the Defense Industrial Base.

same_day.title: US to begin enforcing maritime blockade on Iran on Tuesday
same_day.summary: The U.S. military will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on Iran on Tuesday.

same_day.title: SpaceX targets July 16 for Starship Flight 13, reveals what went wrong on previous launch
same_day.summary: SpaceX is targeting no earlier than July 16 for the next launch of its massive Starship rocket. Flight 13 will be the second launch of the vehicle's Version 3 configuration and will be similar to the mostly successful Flight 12.

same_day.title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
same_day.summary: Astronomers using archival data from Hubble and supportive observations from Webb have located their first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri.

same_day.title: Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle
same_day.summary: Reditus Space, a startup developing recoverable and reusable spacecraft, has completed its first vehicle for launch later this year. The post Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.summary: A new dog collar with GPS tracking enabled by SpaceX's Starlink direct-to-mobile network promises connectivity from almost anywhere.
